title: US attacks Iran in response to attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: US forces launched airstrikes against Iranian positions after commercial ships were attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating regional tensions.

title: IOC lifts participation restrictions on Russia in international competitions
summary: The International Olympic Committee lifted sanctions that prevented Russian athletes from competing, but maintains the ban on the use of the national flag and anthem.

title: World Cup quarterfinal progress: Switzerland, Belgium, and Spain qualify
summary: Switzerland, Belgium, and Spain advanced to the quarterfinals of the World Cup, while the disallowance of a goal for Egypt sparked controversy.

title: NATO summit strengthens collective defense and pressure on Russia
summary: NATO leaders met amid tensions with Russia, agreeing to increase military spending and reaffirming support for Ukraine.

title: Marine Le Pen appeals her embezzlement conviction and announces presidential candidacy
summary: The French far-right leader will appeal the sentence for financial irregularities and confirmed she will run in the presidential election.

title: Acclaimed children's book author Akiko Hayashi dies at 81
summary: The writer and illustrator, known for works such as 'My First Errand' and 'Kon to Aki', died leaving a legacy in Japanese children's literature.

title: Shizuoka governor approves start of maglev train construction
summary: Authorization of the most difficult section of the magnetic levitation train project accelerates the connection between Tokyo and Nagoya, although technical and cost challenges remain.

title: Real estate company Hulic acquires prestigious Tokyo University preparatory school Tetsuryokukai
summary: The Tetsuryokukai educational group, specialized in preparation for the University of Tokyo entrance exam, was bought by real estate company Hulic.

title: Wall Street closes lower due to inflation and oil; SOX index nears bear market
summary: Major New York indexes fell, with the Dow losing 130 points, pressured by rising oil prices and inflation concerns, while the tech sector weakens.

title: Messi scores in nine consecutive World Cup matches as Argentina advances to quarterfinals
summary: Lionel Messi set a record by scoring in nine consecutive World Cup matches, leading Argentina's comeback in the round of 16.

title: Nordic combined excluded from Olympic program starting in 2030
summary: The IOC decided to remove Nordic combined from the Winter Olympics, despite Japan's successes in the discipline.

title: Nikkei index falls more than 1,000 points due to Middle East tensions and rising oil
summary: The Tokyo stock market posted heavy losses, exceeding a drop of 1,000 points, due to geopolitical uncertainty and fears of inflation from higher crude oil costs.

title: Japan posts current account surplus of 3.9 trillion yen in May
summary: The surplus increased 19.5% year-on-year, driven by good performance in the trade balance, although the services deficit widened.

title: Risk of skyscraper collapse in New York forces evacuation of area
summary: A deformed column in a building in downtown Manhattan created a risk of collapse, leading to street closures and evacuation of residents.

title: Japan's southernmost ski resort permanently closes due to renovation costs
summary: The Gokase ski resort in Miyazaki ceased operations due to the inability to afford the necessary investment to upgrade its facilities.

title: Organ trafficking scandal: three people arrested for illegal transplants in Cambodia
summary: An illegal kidney brokering network was dismantled, with arrests in Japan and revelations about patients who traveled to Cambodia to receive transplants in exchange for money.

title: Government and opposition postpone seat reduction to prioritize imperial law reform
summary: The LDP and the Ishin party agreed to postpone the reduction of Diet seats to give priority to debate on reforming the Imperial House Law.

title: Yen weakens to 162 yen per dollar, its lowest level in decades
summary: The Japanese currency fell to the 162 yen range against the dollar, pressured by uncertainty over the monetary policy of the United States and Japan.

title: Government revises economic policy draft to moderate rate hike expectations
summary: The Executive modified the wording of the economic policy guidelines to avoid interpretations that pressure the Bank of Japan to accelerate rate hikes.

title: Cyberattack on KDDI exposes 12 million emails and 7 million passwords
summary: Japanese telecommunications company KDDI suffered a massive leak of customers' personal data, one of the largest security breaches in the country.
